overall_assessment: The bounded RR-AUTH-04 hardening materially improves tenant-public fail-closed auth behavior, reset-token lifecycle, and explicit public-auth risk-matrix coverage, and I did not find a concrete performance regression in the inspected surfaces. Closure should remain blocked on one material contract issue in the touched resolver path: landlord environment payloads can still inherit the tenant fail-closed `effective_methods` view, which conflicts with the bounded package/TODO model that landlord governance may advertise a broader catalog while only tenant-public auth collapses to OTP-first fail-closed behavior. The dispatch packet also has a low-grade reproducibility gap because it points reviewers at a schema path that does not exist at the advertised project-relative location.
recommended_path: Do not close RR-AUTH-04 yet. First separate landlord-environment auth metadata from tenant fail-closed effective-method resolution and add a landlord-host environment regression that keeps metadata aligned with the live landlord password routes; then refresh the final-review package. After that, either correct the dispatch schema reference to the actual Delphi schema location or record that packet-only path issue as explicitly accepted operational debt.

findings.title: Landlord environment auth metadata is derived through tenant fail-closed resolution
findings.rationale: The bounded RR-AUTH-04 TODO/package says landlord public auth may advertise a broader catalog while tenant-public auth collapses fail-closed when no tenant subset is explicitly enabled. But `TenantPublicAuthMethodResolver::currentLandlordGovernance()` simply calls `resolve(rawLandlordSettings, [])`, and `resolveEffectiveMethods()` converts an empty enabled set into `['phone_otp']`. `EnvironmentResolverService` publishes that result on landlord environment payloads. This means landlord-host metadata can advertise a phone-OTP-only effective method set even though the live landlord public auth routes remain password-based, and the touched test coverage shown in the package only proves tenant-environment resolution.
findings.suggested_action: Split landlord catalog reporting from tenant effective-method resolution, or stop exporting landlord `effective_methods` through this fail-closed tenant resolver. Add a landlord-host environment regression that proves metadata and live landlord password routes stay aligned.

findings.title: The final-review dispatch references a schema path that is not present at the advertised location
findings.rationale: The dispatch tells reviewers to return JSON compatible with `schemas/subagent_review_result.schema.json`, but that path does not exist under the bounded project tree. The actual schema used to validate this result lives under `/home/elton/Dev/repos/delphi-ai/schemas/subagent_review_result.schema.json`. A literal no-context reviewer following the packet cannot validate or generate the result without manual path discovery, which weakens the reproducibility of this required review gate.
findings.suggested_action: Update the dispatch/result-contract reference to the real Delphi schema path, or mirror the schema into the referenced project-relative location before treating the packet as self-contained.
